chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR. C.P.No. D - 1629 of 2023. 1.For orders on CMA No.6980/2023. 2.For orders on office objection at Flag.A. 3.For Hearing of main case. - 02.11.2023. Ghulam Shabeer Shar, Advocate, for the petitioner. - 1. Granted. 2. Deferred. 3. The petitioner professes to be a social worker and to have come forward in the public interest whilst praying that this Court may be pleased:- (a). To direct the Respondents to act in accordance with the law as guaranteed under the Constitution of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan, 1973, and also provide basic facilities to the Petitioner and other persons of flood effected areas of Khairpur District. (b). To direct the Respondents to maintain the infrastructure and re-construct the Roads of flood effected areas of Khairpur District, forthwith in order to enable the people of vicinity to travel easily and smoothly run their business or reach at their destinations and also enable the patients of the vicinity to reach at Hospitals and save their precious lives. (c). To direct the Respondents to furnish report before this Honourable Court with documentary evidence that where they have utilized the Funds allocated to them by the orders of this Honourable Court, for rehabilitation and re-construction etc; then in light of their Reports this Honourable court may pass an appropriate order. (d). To grant any other relief, which this Honourable Court deems fit and proper in circumstances of the case. (e). To award the costs of this Petition. Having considered the pleadings, it is apparent that the Petition presents a broad blank canvas, having no definite starting point or end, hence does not constitute a fit case for issuance of a writ under Article 199 of the Constitution.

To award the costs of this Petition. Having considered the pleadings, it is apparent that the Petition presents a broad blank canvas, having no definite starting point or end, hence does not constitute a fit case for issuance of a writ under Article 199 of the Constitution. That being so, the Petition stands dismissed in limine. JUDGE JUDGE Akber.
